message:
fix(windows): gate SSH and Unix-only code for cross-platform builds

- Create transfer_stub.rs with cross-platform utilities (project_id_from_path,
  parse_rchignore) and swap via cfg_attr on non-Unix
- Gate workers_probe and workers_benchmark with cfg(unix) stubs
- Add PlatformError import for non-Unix workers.rs stubs
- Fix hook_windows.rs release_worker signature (CommandTimingBreakdown param)
- Add thread-local mock override to prevent parallel test race conditions
- Gate TransferPipeline.ssh_options field and SSH usage blocks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2q3b","title":"Test: Circuit Breaker Trip & Recovery","description":"## Purpose
Test circuit breaker behavior with real workers - tripping on failures, staying open, and recovering when workers become healthy.

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger for all phases:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_circuit_trip","phase":"induce_failure","msg":"Inducing failures","data":{"worker":"css","failure_count":0}}
{"ts":"...","level":"WARN","test":"test_circuit_trip","phase":"circuit_state","msg":"Circuit state change","data":{"worker":"css","previous":"closed","new":"open","failures":5}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_circuit_recovery","phase":"probe","msg":"Half-open probe","data":{"worker":"css","probe_result":"success"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_circuit_recovery","phase":"circuit_state","msg":"Circuit recovered","data":{"worker":"css","previous":"half_open","new":"closed"}}
```

## Test Cases

### Circuit Tripping
1. Consecutive failures trip circuit:
   - Cause N consecutive failures to worker
   - Verify: circuit transitions to OPEN
   - Verify: requests no longer sent to that worker
   - Log: failure count, state transitions

2. Half-open probing:
   - Wait for circuit timeout
   - Verify: circuit enters HALF_OPEN
   - Verify: probe request sent
   - Log: timeout, probe attempt

3. Recovery on success:
   - Fix worker, send probe
   - Verify: circuit closes on success
   - Verify: worker receives traffic again
   - Log: probe result, state change

### Circuit State Persistence
4. Daemon restart preserves state:
   - Trip circuit, restart daemon
   - Verify: circuit state preserved (or reasonably recovered)
   - Log: state before/after restart

5. Multi-worker circuits:
   - Trip circuit for one worker
   - Verify: other workers unaffected
   - Verify: traffic routed to healthy workers
   - Log: per-worker circuit state

### Error Counting
6. Transient errors do not trip:
   - Single error followed by success
   - Verify: circuit stays closed
   - Log: error count, reset

7. Timeout errors count:
   - Slow worker causes timeouts
   - Verify: timeouts count toward circuit trip
   - Log: timeout as failure

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Circuit breaker trips on real failures
- [ ] Half-open probing works
- [ ] Recovery on success
- [ ] Multi-worker isolation correct
- [ ] ALL state transitions logged with structured JSON","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:57.413169248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:31:53.147449028Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:31:53.147194338Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-23n3 (Error Handling & Recovery tests include circuit breaker).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q3b","depends_on_id":"bd-23n3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:09.723264950Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q3b","depends_on_id":"bd-ccqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:10.891090892Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qgh","title":"Fix true_e2e failopen tests locating rch/rchd binaries","description":"tests/true_e2e/failopen_tests.rs computes project_root() by walking up two parents from CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR (which is .../rch for these tests), yielding /data/projects instead of the workspace root. This makes the suite try to spawn target/debug/rchd from the wrong directory (/data/projects/target/debug/rchd) and fail-open tests abort immediately. Fix project_root() to resolve the workspace root correctly and re-run cargo test -p rch --test true_e2e.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-26T22:45:50.411807212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:13:13.460941274Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:13:13.460877906Z","close_reason":"Fixed true_e2e failopen tests to locate workspace root correctly (CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR parent) so rch/rchd binaries are found; stabilized daemon recovery + normalization/fixture issues; true_e2e suite now passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2s60","title":"Fix pre_tool_use line detection false positive","description":"`rch/src/agent/hook.rs`: `is_pre_tool_use_line` incorrectly returns true for lines like `pre_tool_uses = "rch"`, causing `agent::hook::tests::test_is_pre_tool_use_line` to fail. Tighten matcher to require exact key `pre_tool_use` followed by whitespace and '=' (or '=' directly) and ignore supersets like `pre_tool_uses`/`pre_tool_useful`.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-26T22:37:27.416695435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:13:10.591102636Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:13:10.591038016Z","close_reason":"Tightened pre_tool_use key matching to require key boundary (whitespace/=) so pre_tool_uses/pre_tool_useful no longer match; updated unit test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2s8m","title":"E2E Test: Multi-Worker Scenarios","description":"Add end-to-end tests for multi-worker fleet operations AND worker lifecycle behaviors. This bead now consolidates the overlapping lifecycle scenarios to reduce duplication and provide a single canonical, comprehensive E2E suite for fleet + worker lifecycle.

## Scenarios to Test
### 1) Load Balancing (multi-worker)
- Verify jobs distributed across workers
- Verify speed-score weighted selection
- Verify slot-count awareness

### 2) Worker Prioritization
- High-priority worker preferred
- Fallback to lower-priority when busy
- Priority changes take effect immediately

### 3) Cached Project Locality
- Repeat builds prefer same worker
- Cache invalidation triggers re-transfer
- Cross-worker build correctness

### 4) Fleet Scaling
- Add worker mid-session
- Remove worker mid-session
- Fleet down to single worker

### 5) Heterogeneous Workers
- Mix of Rust-only and Bun-only workers
- Capability-based routing
- Toolchain mismatch handling

### 6) Worker Registration & Re-Registration (lifecycle)
- Fresh worker joins fleet
- Worker re-registration after restart
- Duplicate worker ID handling

### 7) Worker Health Monitoring (lifecycle)
- Health check success path
- Health check timeout handling
- Worker marked unhealthy after N failures
- Worker recovery from unhealthy state

### 8) Worker Removal & Disconnects (lifecycle)
- Graceful worker shutdown
- Unexpected worker disconnect
- In-flight job handling during disconnect

### 9) Worker Updates (lifecycle)
- Capability update after toolchain install
- Priority adjustment
- Slot count changes

## Test Infrastructure
- Minimum 3 Docker worker containers (preferred)
- OR localhost SSH workers for lifecycle tests
- Configurable capabilities per worker
- Metrics collection for verification
- Each scenario isolated and idempotent

## Logging Format
```
[E2E] TEST START: load_balance_distribution
[E2E]   Fleet: worker-1 (8 slots), worker-2 (4 slots), worker-3 (8 slots)
[E2E]   Jobs submitted: 20
[E2E]   Distribution: w1=9, w2=4, w3=7
[E2E]   Expected (by slots): w1=8, w2=4, w3=8
[E2E]   Variance: within 15% tolerance
[E2E] TEST PASS: load_balance_distribution (45.2s)
```

## Success Criteria
- Distribution matches slot-weighted expectation (±15%)
- Worker lifecycle scenarios pass in CI
- Each test < 30s (where applicable)
- No flaky tests

## Edge Cases & UX
- Worker removal during active job should not corrupt state.
- Duplicate worker IDs are handled deterministically with clear errors.
- Capability mismatch yields warnings, not crashes.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rchd/src/workers.rs: lifecycle transitions (register, update, remove).
- rchd/src/health.rs: unhealthy -> recovery transitions.
- rchd/src/selection.rs: prioritization + heterogeneous capability routing.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-2s8m.sh: consolidated fleet + lifecycle scenarios.
- JSONL logging with per-scenario timing + summary.
